title: Building the 800 VDC Ecosystem for Efficient, Scalable AI Factories
claim_supported: Rack power approaching 1MW/rack; capacitors/supercapacitors near racks absorb high-frequency GPU spikes; named ecosystem: ABB, Eaton, GE Vernova, Hitachi Energy, Schneider, Siemens, Vertiv, Delta.
locator: Article body, energy-storage/buffer paragraph: 'High-power capacitors and supercapacitors are placed close to the compute racks'
notes: Refreshes/extends atlas L02-043 (same publisher, later 2025 post). Vendor technical blog describing the ecosystem/architecture, not itself a buyer procurement artifact -- used as technical/timeline context, not primary demand evidence.

india_origin_audit.notes: US public company, HQ Santa Clara, CA.

title: Vicor Introduces a New 800V Bus Converter Module
claim_supported: BCM4414 800V bus converter: 500-800V input, 1.6kW, 97.7% peak efficiency, integrated transient/EMI protection in a VIA package.
notes: 2019 product predates AI-rack application (stated markets were industrial/military). Establishes Vicor (already atlas L02-113 context) as an incumbent dense-power-module maker capable of extending into the buffer/transient-smoothing space -- the exact kill-trigger risk named in the idea's own fix_applied_notes.

india_origin_audit.notes: US public company, HQ Andover, MA.

title: 巴拿马供电技术白皮书 (Panama Power Supply Technology White Paper), doc. ODCC-2020-02001
authors_or_org: Open Data Center Committee (ODCC), Alibaba-led working group
claim_supported: ODCC formally chartered the Alibaba-led 'Panama Power' DC-UPS project (Nov 2019) and published white paper ODCC-2020-02001 (2020-04-10), naming DC power as a formal hyperscale procurement category in China.
locator: PDF properties confirm a 59-page zh-CN document at this host; title/number/date corroborated against ODCC meeting-notice page and independent CN industry commentary citing the identical document ID.
notes: Binary PDF fetched successfully (3.7MB, 59pp, zh-CN) but is image/compressed and not machine-OCR-extractable this session; body-text claims are corroborated via secondary CN sources citing the same document number/date rather than directly quoted. Tier lowered to T3 pending a clean re-fetch. Historical (2020) -- used only as CN standards-body demand foundation, NOT counted toward the 2030-timing requirement.

institutions.name: Open Data Center Committee / Alibaba Group

india_origin_audit.notes: China-based industry standards consortium (ODCC), led by Alibaba.

title: 800V HVDC商用元年：国产MCU破解AIDC供电转型难题 (2026: The First Year of 800V HVDC Commercialization -- Domestic MCUs Address AIDC Power-Transition Challenges)
claim_supported: ByteDance announced large-scale 800V HVDC adoption in early 2025; China's 'East Data West Computing' program made 800V power a mandatory requirement for new AIDC construction; 2026 named industry 'first year of 800V HVDC commercialization.'
locator: Article body: '2026年，一度被业内视作800V HVDC商用元年' paragraph naming ByteDance and 东数西算
notes: Published by a component vendor (GigaDevice) marketing its own MCU line -- vendor framing of product superiority is not independent validation, but the named buyer (ByteDance) and government program (East Data West Computing) are independently checkable public facts.

title: Heron Power raises $140M to ramp production of grid-altering tech
claim_supported: Heron Link: 5MW-per-unit SST converting MV to 800VDC for Nvidia reference racks, 30s onboard battery backup; $178M total raised ($38M Series A + $140M Series B); >40GW customer pipeline (~1/3 datacenter); 40GW/yr factory planned, pilot production early 2027.
locator: Article body: funding, product (Heron Link), and customer-pipeline paragraphs
notes: Well-funded, near-identical competitor to C-03's proposed 2.4MW MV-to-800VDC SST block, moving on a commercial (non-certification-first) timeline. Material red-team finding for C-03's standards-first thesis.

india_origin_audit.notes: US startup (San Francisco Bay Area); investors Andreessen Horowitz, Breakthrough Energy Ventures, Capricorn, etc., all US/multinational.

title: Hyperscale Power is the latest startup to challenge 140-year-old transformer tech
claim_supported: €5M seed (World Fund, Vsquared Ventures, March 2026); ETH Zürich-derived high-frequency SST tech (99.1% efficiency claim) targeting MV-to-LVDC datacenter conversion; no named customers yet.
locator: Article body: funding and founder/technology paragraphs
notes: A second, earlier-stage European entrant into the same MV-SST-for-datacenter niche C-03 targets -- reinforces that competitive entry is happening well before any IEC-certification-gated 2028 milestone.

india_origin_audit.notes: Founder holds ETH Zürich PhD; investors World Fund and Vsquared Ventures are German climate-tech VCs.

title: Enphase Energy Joins Open Compute Project as Platinum Member to Help Advance AI Data Center Power Standards
claim_supported: Enphase joined OCP as a Platinum member on 2026-06-30 specifically to help standardize ±400VDC/800VDC AI-datacenter power architectures, contributing its IQ SST product line to almost 700 OCP member organizations.
locator: Press release body, membership/purpose paragraph and CEO quotes
notes: Confirms OCP's SST/800VDC standards track is actively advancing with public-company vendor commitment -- partial, vendor-side evidence toward C-03's binding 2028 'OCP SST procurement signal' gate. Not yet a hyperscaler RFI or spec revision naming SST power units, so the gate condition is not yet fully met by this alone.

india_origin_audit.notes: US public company, HQ Fremont, CA.

title: IEC 60076-24:2020 Power transformers -- Part 24: Specification of voltage-regulating distribution transformers
claim_supported: Edition 1.0 published 2020-07-09; status = Published; stability date 2029; scope = voltage-regulating distribution transformers (25kVA-3150kVA, up to 36kV) -- not high-frequency SST insulation coordination. No amendment/new edition in development listed.
locator: IEC Webstore publication record, status/stability-date/scope fields
notes: CRITICAL RED-TEAM FINDING: the idea's binding 2028 hard gate requires 'demonstrable IEC 60076-24-class standard progress (committee draft or later)', but IEC 60076-24 is a published, stable-through-2029 standard covering a different device class (voltage-regulating distribution transformers) than the MV-SST/HF-transformer insulation-coordination problem the idea describes. A targeted search this session found no IEC TC14 (or other) new work item specifically addressing SST high-frequency insulation coordination. As currently written, the gate's named instrument shows no observable 'progress' and may never trigger on its own terms -- this should be corrected or the gate re-anchored to a real in-progress work item (e.g., IEC/IEEE SST-specific standardization efforts, if any exist) before 2028.

title: Accelsius is Building for a Two-Phase Future
authors_or_org: Data Center Richness (independent newsletter), reporting Accelsius, Inc. Series B
claim_supported: Accelsius closed $65M Series B (Jan 12 2026, led by Johnson Controls, with Legrand); NeuCool IR150 rack delivers up to 150kW/42U two-phase direct-to-chip cooling; named customer DarkNX (300MW Ontario campus); fluid PFAS-status not disclosed in this article.
locator: Newsletter body, funding and product paragraphs
notes: Competitor evidence for C-04's two-phase cooling niche; do not assume Accelsius fluid is PFAS-free -- unconfirmed. Also evidences a growing multi-vendor qualification burden relevant to C-05's metrology thesis.

india_origin_audit.notes: Accelsius HQ Austin, TX; publisher is a US-based independent industry newsletter.

title: 国家发展改革委等部门关于印发《数据中心绿色低碳发展专项行动计划》的通知 (Notice on the Data Center Green and Low-Carbon Development Special Action Plan)
authors_or_org: National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC); Ministry of Industry and Information Technology; National Data Administration; National Energy Administration (China)
claim_supported: By end-2025, newly built/expanded large & super-large data centers must reach PUE ≤ 1.25; national hub-node data center projects must reach PUE ≤ 1.2 (NDRC/MIIT joint notice, July 2024).
locator: PDF confirmed as an official NDRC document dated July 2024 (creation metadata matches); specific PUE figures corroborated via independent secondary industry summaries citing this exact document by title/date/issuer -- the scanned/compressed PDF body text could not be OCR-extracted directly this session.
notes: PDF fetched (binary, ~270KB, confirmed Chinese-language government document) but not machine-readable this session; figures are corroborated-but-not-directly-quoted. Recommend a clean OCR re-fetch before final scoring. Supports both C-04 (PFAS-free two-phase cooling, PUE pressure) and C-05 (conformance-testing demand) CN legs.

title: Nidec Accelerates Liquid-Cooling Adoption for AI Generation Data Centers: Prototypes Project Deschutes CDU Based on Google OCP Specification
claim_supported: Google open-sourced the 'Project Deschutes' CDU design specification to selected/certified vendors; Nidec's compliant prototype delivers 2MW-class cooling capacity at 80 PSI with IEEE-519 ultra-low-harmonic-distortion VFD; exhibited at SC25 (Nov 2025).
locator: Press release body, spec/capacity paragraph, dated 2025-03-12 (event: SC25 Nov 2025)
notes: Confirms a hyperscale buyer (Google) authored a concrete, dated, vendor-facing hardware spec with no independent acceptance-test/qualification standard behind it -- directly supports C-05's core demand thesis. Japan-HQ vendor (Nidec) building to a US buyer spec; JP counted only as secondary-market technical context, not a US/CN substitute.

title: Companies show off Google-inspired Project Deschutes CDUs
claim_supported: Eight named vendors (Boyd, CoolerMaster, Delta, Envicool, Nidec, nVent, Stulz, Vertiv) built CDUs to Google's Project Deschutes spec: 2MW capacity, 3°C approach temperature difference, 80 PSI.
locator: Article body: vendor list and spec paragraph, dated 2025-10-17
notes: Independent secondary confirmation of the Nidec primary (P3R2-C-05-S01); also names Envicool (Chinese vendor) as Deschutes-compliant, linking the US-spec-driven qualification-demand thesis to the CN vendor ecosystem C-05 also targets.

title: The ‘One Big Beautiful Bill Act’ Substantially Alters Clean Energy Tax Landscape
claim_supported: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (H.R.1, signed July 2025) accelerated the Section 45V clean-hydrogen production credit's construction-start deadline to December 31, 2027; 45V is exempted from the new FEOC restrictions applied to most other surviving energy credits.
locator: Article body, Section 45V paragraph: 'accelerates the date by which clean hydrogen production projects must begin construction to December 31, 2027'
notes: Material refresh of the atlas's hydrogen-policy baseline: mid-2025 legislation shortens the US 45V-subsidized construction window well inside C-07's 2026-2029 pre-company period, narrowing (but not eliminating, given the two-year extension secured in negotiation) the H2Hubs-linked demand runway and reinforcing the idea's own electrowinning-first hedge.

india_origin_audit.notes: US law firm, HQ Houston, TX.

title: Ingeteam unveils a new rectifier solution for electrolysers
claim_supported: INGECON H2 FSK E12000: IGBT-based active-front-end rectifier, 12,000A nominal (up to 15,860A max), harmonic distortion index <3% with no added filters/STATCOM needed; first units delivered September 2023 to German and Spanish green-hydrogen plants.
locator: Press release body, spec table, dated 2023-03-31
notes: A European incumbent already sells the exact AFE/IGBT rectifier class C-07 proposes -- but for new-build plants, not retrofit of an installed thyristor fleet. Narrows (does not eliminate) C-07's differentiation to retrofit-of-existing-base plus stack-neutral performance contracts, since the core AFE-vs-thyristor technology gap is already commercially served for new plants.
